soru: Which approximate wavelength is used for most airborne weather radar systems?
secenekler: 3 cm
9 cm
23 cm
11 mm
dogru: 3 cm

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Complete the following statement.  Aircraft Surface Movement Radar operates on frequencies in the (i) .......... Band employing an antenna that rotates at approximately (ii) .......... Revolutions per minute; it is (iii) ......... Possible to determine the type of aircraft from the return on the radar screen.
(i) EHF     (ii) 100     (iii) never
(i) EHF    (ii) 30       (iii) never
(i) SHF     (ii) 60       (iii) sometimes
dogru: (i) SHF     (ii) 60       (iii) sometimes

soru:
(i) SHF    (ii) 10      (iii) always
In a primary pulse radar you have:
secenekler: A directional aerial for both transmission and reception
An omni-directional aerial for transmission and directional aerial for reception
A directional aerial for transmission and another one for reception
A directional aerial for transmission and an omni-directional aerial for reception
dogru: A directional aerial for both transmission and reception

soru: A radio altimeter employing a continuous wave signal would have:
secenekler: An omni-directional aerial for transmission and directional aerial for reception
A directional aerial for transmission and an omni-directional aerial for reception
A directional aerial for both transmission and reception
A directional aerial for transmission and another one for reception
dogru: A directional aerial for transmission and another one for reception

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: A primary radar operates on the principle of…
pulse technique.
continuous wave transmission.
phase comparison.
dogru: pulse technique.

soru:
transponder interrogation.
Which of the following types of radar systems are most suited for short range operation?
secenekler: Primary continuous wave
Millimetric pulse
Centimetric pulse
Secondary continuous wave
dogru: Primary continuous wave

soru: Which one of the following statements is correct concerning the use in primary radar of continuous wave transmissions as compared with pulse transmissions?
secenekler: It is less effective in short range radars but more effective in long range radars
The equipment required is more complex in continuous wave radar but this is offset by greater reliability and accuracy
It eliminates the minimum target reception range
A smaller common transmitter and receiver aerial can be used
dogru: It eliminates the minimum target reception range

soru: Assuming sufficient transmission power, what is the maximum range of a ground radar with a pulse repetition frequency of 450 pulses per second, given that the velocity of light is 300 000 km/s?
secenekler: 150 km
333 km
666 km
dogru: 333 km

soru: A radar facility transmitting at a Pulse Recurrence Frequency (PRF) of 1200 pulses/second will have a maximum unambiguous range of approximately…
secenekler: 27 NM.
67 NM.
dogru: 67 NM.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The main factor which determines the minimum range that can be measured by a pulsed radar is…
pulse length.
pulse frequency.
pulse amplitude.
dogru: pulse length.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: pulse repetition rate.
Ignoring pulse length, the maximum pulse repetition frequency (PRF) that can be used by a primary radar facility to detect targets unambiguously to a range of 200 NM is…(pps = pulses per second)
405 pps.
810 pps.
dogru: 405 pps.

soru:
308 pps.
782 pps.
secenekler: The maximum pulse repetition frequency (PRF) that can be used by a primary radar facility in order to detect targets unambiguously at a range of 50 NM is…(pps = pulses per second)
610 pps.
713 pps.

soru:
3240 pps.
Disregarding pulse length and fly-back, what is the Pulse Repetition Frequency(PRF) of a radar designed to have a maximum unambiguous range of 50 km?
secenekler: 6000 pulses per second.
3000 pulses per second.
167 pulses per second.
330 pulses per second.
dogru: 3000 pulses per second.

soru: Which combination of characteristics gives best screen picture in a primary search radar?
secenekler: Short pulse length and wide beam
Long pulse length and wide beam
Short pulse length and narrow beam
Long pulse length and narrow beam
dogru: Short pulse length and narrow beam

soru: The minimum range of a primary radar, using the pulse technique, is determined by the (i)........ ; the maximum unambiguous range by the (ii).........
secenekler: pulse length (ii) length of the timebase
transmission frequency (ii) transmitter power output
transmission frequency (ii) pulse repetition frequency
pulse length (ii) pulse repetition frequency
dogru: pulse length (ii) pulse repetition frequency

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: A ground radar transmitting at a PRF of 1200 pulses/second will have a maximum unambiguous range of approximately…
67 NM.
dogru: 67 NM.

soru:
27 NM.
The maximum range of primary radar depends on…
secenekler: pulse length.
wave length.
frequency.
pulse repetition frequency.
dogru: pulse repetition frequency.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: To double the effective range of a primary radar, the power output must be increased by a factor of…

soru:
2.
The prime factor in determining the maximum unambiguous range of a primary radar is the…
secenekler: power output.
size of the parabolic receiver aerial.
height of the transmitter above the ground.
pulse repetition frequency.
dogru: pulse repetition frequency.

soru: In relation to primary radar, what does the term Pulse Recurrence Frequency signify?
secenekler: The radar frequency used.
The time between each transmission of pulses.
The number of pulses transmitted per second.
The number of revolutions performed by the radar antenna per minute.
dogru: The number of pulses transmitted per second.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The maximum range obtainable from an ATC en route surveillance radar is approximately…

soru: 50 to 100 NM.
secenekler: In relation to radar systems that use pulse technology, the term ‘Pulse Recurrence Frequency (PRF)’ signifies the…
delay after which the process re-starts.
ratio of pulse period to pulse width.
number of pulses per second.
dogru: number of pulses per second.

soru:
number of cycles per second.
Which of the following equipment uses primary radar principles?
secenekler: Global Positioning System (GPS)
Airborne weather radar (AWR)
Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR)
Distance Measuring Equipment (DME)
dogru: Airborne weather radar (AWR)

soru: The theoretical maximum range for an Airborne Weather Radar is determined by the…
secenekler: beam width.
carrier wave frequency.
pulse length.
pulse repetition frequency.
dogru: pulse repetition frequency.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: In a primary radar using pulse technique, pulse length determines…
beam width.
minimum theoretical range.
maximum theoretical range.
dogru: minimum theoretical range.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: bearing resolution.
In a primary radar using pulse technique, pulse repetition frequency determines…
maximum theoretical range.
target discrimination.
dogru: maximum theoretical range.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: minimum range.
beam width.
In a primary radar using pulse technique, the ability to discriminate between targets in azimuth is a factor of…
beam width.
dogru: beam width.

soru:
pulse length.
pulse recurrence rate.
aerial rotation rate.
secenekler: Which of the following types of radar equipment operate by means of the pulse technique?1. Aerodrome Surface Movement Radar2. Airborne Weather Radar3. Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR)4. Aerodrome Surveillance -approach- Radar
2, 3 and 4 only
2 and 4 only

soru:
1, 2 and 4 only
Which type of radar gives an indication of the shape and, to some extent, the type of aircraft producing a return?
secenekler: Aerodrome Surface Movement Radar (ASMR)
Airborne Weather Radar (AWR)
Aerodrome Surveillance Radar
Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR)
dogru: Aerodrome Surface Movement Radar (ASMR)

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: When an operator increases the range on a radar-display, in general,
both the PRF and the pulse length increase.
both the PRF and the pulse length decrease.
the PRF becomes lower and the pulse length larger.
dogru: the PRF becomes lower and the pulse length larger.

soru:
the PRF becomes higher and the pulse length smaller.
What information may be displayed on an ATC radar screen connected only to a primary radar system?
secenekler: Aircraft position, SSR code and altitude.
Aircraft altitude.
Aircraft position only.
Aircraft position and SSR code.
dogru: Aircraft position only.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Echoes that do not change in distance from the antenna (relative speed zero) of a ground radar with a Moving Target Indicator (MTI) are dangerous for the following reason
The frequency of the transmitted radar signals changes too much by the Doppler effect.
The radar is not able to display two echoes moving in the same direction.
The moving target indicator (MTI) eliminates such echoes.
dogru: The moving target indicator (MTI) eliminates such echoes.

soru:
The MTI does not recognise this as a moving object due to the blind speed
What type of beam is used by Airborne Weather Radar in weather mode?
secenekler: 3 – 5º Pencil beam
85º Fan-shaped beam
85º Pencil beam
3º Fan-shaped beam
dogru: 3 – 5º Pencil beam

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Airborne weather radar systems use a wavelength of approximately 3 cm in order to…
obtain optimum use of the cosecant squared beam.
detect larger water droplets.
detect small as well as large cloud formations.
dogru: detect larger water droplets.

soru:
transmit at a higher pulse repetition frequency for extended range.
In the MAPPING MODE the airborne weather radar utilises a:
secenekler: Pencil beam to a maximum range of 60 NM
Pencil beam effective from zero to 150 NM
Fan shaped beam effective up to a maximum of 50 NM to 60 NM
Fan shaped beam effective up to a range of 150 NM
dogru: Fan shaped beam effective up to a maximum of 50 NM to 60 NM

soru: A frequency of 10 GHz is considered to be the optimum for use in an airborne weather radar system because…
secenekler: static interference is minimised.
greater detail can be obtained at more distant ranges for smaller water droplets.
it enables the weather radar to detect clear air turbulence
larger water droplets give more distinct echoes.
dogru: larger water droplets give more distinct echoes.

soru: Which of the following is a complete list of airborne weather radar antenna stabilisation axes?
secenekler: roll and pitch
roll, pitch and yaw
pitch and yaw
roll and yaw
dogru: roll and pitch

soru: In which mode of operation does the aircraft weather radar  use a cosecant radiation pattern?
secenekler: MAPPING
MANUAL
WEATHER
CONTOUR
dogru: MAPPING

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The frequency of airborne weather radar is…

soru:
9375 GHz.
In weather radar the use of a cosecant beam in 'Mapping' mode enables:
secenekler: A greater radar range to be achieved
Scanning of a large ground zone producing echos whose signals are practically independent of distance
Higher definition echoes to be produced giving a clearer picture
Better reception of echos on contrasting terrain such as ground to sea
dogru: Scanning of a large ground zone producing echos whose signals are practically independent of distance

soru: In which frequency band do most airborne weather radars operate?
secenekler: EHF
VHF
UHF
SHF
dogru: SHF

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The main factors that determine cloud detection by airborne weather radar are the…
size of the water drops and diameter of the radar scanner.
range from cloud, wavelength and frequency used.
size of the water drops, wavelength and frequency used.
dogru: size of the water drops, wavelength and frequency used.

soru:
rotational speed of the radar scanner and range from cloud.
Which of the following phenomena is least likely to be detected by radar?
secenekler: Precipitation
Clear air turbulence
Wet snow and turbulence in cloud that has precipitation
Turbulence in cloud that has precipitation
dogru: Clear air turbulence

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Airborne weather radars are generally based on the use of…
secondary radar in the VHF band.
secondary radar in the SHF band.
primary radar in the SHF band.
dogru: primary radar in the SHF band.

soru:
primary radar in the UHF band.
Which statement relating to the stabilization of airborne weather radar antennae is true?
secenekler: The pilot can choose the axes of stabilization with the system’s stabilization selector switch.
They are stabilized with respect to the pitch, roll, and yaw-axis.
They are stabilized with respect to the pitch and roll axis but not with respect to the yaw-axis.
They are stabilized with respect to the yaw-axis, but not with respect to the pitch and roll axis.
dogru: They are stabilized with respect to the pitch and roll axis but not with respect to the yaw-axis.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Airborne Weather Radar has been primarily developed to detect
the kinds of precipitation which are usually accompanied by turbulence.
areas of wind sheer and severe aircraft icing.
all kinds of frozen precipitation such as hail, snow and graupel.
dogru: the kinds of precipitation which are usually accompanied by turbulence.

soru:
areas of severe clear air turbulence.
The antennae of modern airborne weather radars are stabilized by means of:
secenekler: artificial gravity switches.
mercury switches.
inputs from the aircraft’s attitude system.
feedback from the antenna accelerometers.
dogru: inputs from the aircraft’s attitude system.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: On an airborne weather radar display, the areas of greatest turbulence are usually indicated on the screen by…
colour zones of red and magenta.
colour zones of green and yellow.
areas where no colour is shown.
dogru: colour zones of red and magenta.

soru:
colour zones of blue and orange.
In an Airborne Weather Radar that has a colour cathode display, increasing severity of rain and turbulence is generally shown by a change of colour from:
secenekler: yellow to amber to blue
yellow to orange to red
green to yellow to red
green to red to black
dogru: green to yellow to red

soru: In general the operation of airborne weather radar equipment on the ground is:
secenekler: Totally prohibited
Only permitted with certain precautions, to safeguard health of personnel and to protect equipment
Unrestrictedly permitted in aerodrome maintenance areas
Permitted anywhere
dogru: Only permitted with certain precautions, to safeguard health of personnel and to protect equipment

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: A weather radar, set to the 100 NM scale, shows a squall between 40 NM and 50 NM.By changing the scale to 50 NM, the return on the radar screen should…
decrease in area but not change in position on the screen.
decrease in area and move to the top of the screen.
increase in area and move to the top of the screen.
dogru: increase in area and move to the top of the screen.

soru:
increase in area and appear nearer to the bottom of the screen.
The pencil shaped beam of airborne weather radar is used in preference to the mapping mode for the determination of ground features:
secenekler: Beyond 150 NM because the wider beam gives better definition
Beyond 50 to 60 NM because more power can be concentrated in the narrower beam
When approaching coast-lines in polar regions
Beyond 100 NM because insufficient antenna tilt angle is available with the mapping mode
dogru: Beyond 50 to 60 NM because more power can be concentrated in the narrower beam

soru: In order to ascertain whether a cloud return on an Aircraft Weather Radar (AWR) is at or above the height of the aircraft, the tilt control should be set to: (Assume a beam width of 5°)
secenekler: 5° up
2.5° down

soru: Which of the following is correct concerning the setting of tilt angle on a weather radar when it is being used to determine the presence of weather at, or above, your current altitude?
secenekler: The tilt angle should be lower when the range is increased
The tilt angle should be lower when the aircraft is climbing
The tilt angle should be higher when the aircraft is climbing
The tilt angle should be higher when the range is increased
dogru: The tilt angle should be lower when the aircraft is climbing

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The gain control knob of an AWR adjusts the…
automatic gain control of the AWR is activated.
receiver sensitivity in order to achieve optimum target acquisition.
brightness of the display.
dogru: receiver sensitivity in order to achieve optimum target acquisition.

soru:
power level of the transmitted energy is made dependent on the selected range.
Refer to image The Magnetic Heading of an aircraft is 040°. On the Airborne Weather Radar display the relative bearing of and the distance to the most southerly part of Lands End (approximate position: 50°03'N , 005°40'W) are, 030°R and 80 NM. What is the position of the aircraft based on these observations? The slant range correction and the map convergency between aircraft position and Lands End may be neglected.
View Annex 062-600097
What cloud type is most easily detected by airborne weather radar?
secenekler: Altostratus
Stratus
Cirrocumulus
Cumulonimbus
dogru: Cumulonimbus

soru: Which of the following lists the phenomena least likely to be detected by radar?
secenekler: Wet snow and turbulence in cloud that has precipitation
Clear air turbulence
Precipitation
Turbulence in cloud that has precipitation
dogru: Clear air turbulence

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The heaviest turbulence is likely to be encountered…
where the area of heaviest precipitation is closest to the edge of the thunderstorm cell.
in the very core of a thunderstorm cell.
about halfway between two thunderstorm cells.
dogru: where the area of heaviest precipitation is closest to the edge of the thunderstorm cell.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: in the area of heaviest precipitation, usually at the core of the thunderstorm cell.
When using airborne weather radar in the mapping mode in polar areas one runs the risk of…
mistaking the edge of coastal ice off shore for the real coastline.
underestimating distances because the cold seawater is causing super-refraction.
dogru: mistaking the edge of coastal ice off shore for the real coastline.

soru:
getting a distorted picture because of ice reflection.
aurora borealis -polar light- causing false returns.
Refer to the annex.  The pictures  show an aircraft’s position in relation to thunderstorm cells, and the image of the pilot’s radar. To detect the blind alley the pilot should…
View Annex 062-600164
secenekler: select the cosecant square beam for better alley detection.
switch over from normal to weather mode.
increase the range setting of the radar.
initiate a steep climb.
dogru: increase the range setting of the radar.

soru: The selection of code 2000 on an aircraft SSR transponder indicates:
secenekler: Unlawful interference with the planned operation of the flight
Transponder malfunction
Entry into airspace from an area where SSR operation has not been required
An emergency
dogru: Entry into airspace from an area where SSR operation has not been required

soru: Which of the following Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) codes is used to indicate transponder malfunction?
Which one of the following Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) codes should be used by aircraft entering airspace from an area where SSR operation has not been required?
A TCAS II equiped aircraft will have Mode S because:
secenekler: The datalink is required to co-ordinate evasive manoeuvres.
Mode S transmits a 3D position.
True altitude is obtained from Mode S.
The data link is required to transmit relative position.
dogru: The datalink is required to co-ordinate evasive manoeuvres.

soru: Why is a secondary radar display screen free of storm clutter?
secenekler: The frequencies used are too low to give returns from moisture sources.
A moving target indicator facility suppresses the display of static or near-static returns.
Echo returns are not used in secondary radar.
The frequencies used are too high to give returns from moisture sources.
dogru: Echo returns are not used in secondary radar.

soru: Which one of the following is an advantage of a secondary radar system when compared to a primary radar system?
secenekler: The relatively small ground antenna transmits no side lobes, thus eliminating the danger of false replies from the airborne transponder.
Possibility of obtaining speed information for aircraft within range.
Is not limited to line of sight.
The required power of transmission from the ground equipment is reduced.
dogru: The required power of transmission from the ground equipment is reduced.

soru: What additional information is transmitted when Mode C is selected on the aircraft’s SSR transponder?
secenekler: Altitude based on regional QNH.
Pressure altitude based on 1013.25 hPa.
Aircraft height based on sub-scale setting.
Height based on QFE.
dogru: Pressure altitude based on 1013.25 hPa.

soru: Ground Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) equipment incorporates a transmitter and receiver that operate in the following frequencies:
secenekler: transmits on 1030 MHz and receives on 1090 MHz.
transmits on 1090 MHz and receives on 1030 MHz.
transmits and receives on 1090 MHz.
transmits and receives on 1030 MHz.
dogru: transmits on 1030 MHz and receives on 1090 MHz.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The frequency of an SSR ground transmission is…

soru:
1120 MHz.
The ATC transponder system, excluding Mode S, contains :
secenekler: Four modes, each 4096 codes
Two modes, each 4096 codes
Two modes, each 1024 codes
Four modes, each 1024 codes
dogru: Two modes, each 4096 codes

soru: A secondary radar can provide up to 4096 different codes. These 4096 codes can be used in:
secenekler: All modes
Mode A only
Mode S
Mode C only
dogru: All modes

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The code transmitted by a SSR transponder consists of…
pulses.
amplitude differences.
frequency differences.
dogru: pulses.

soru:
phase differences.
What is the maximum number of Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) transponder codes in Mode A?
What equipment works on the interrogator/transponder principle?
secenekler: Aerodrome Surface Movement Radar
Global Positioning System (GPS)
Airborne Weather Radar (AWR)
Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR)
dogru: Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR)

soru: How does a Mode S interrogator identify aircraft?
secenekler: The four letter SELCAL code
An eight bit identifier, which gives 212 unique combinations
A 24 bit identifier, giving over 16 million combinations
The P3 pulse
dogru: A 24 bit identifier, giving over 16 million combinations

soru: Which statement with respect to interrogation modes is correct?
secenekler: Mode S Selective will trigger replies of all mode S transponders. Mode A/C transponders are suppressed.
Intermode A/C/S all call and Intermode A/C only all call, differ in the pulse spacing used between the P1 and P3 pulse.
Mode S Broadcast will transmit information to all mode S transponders.
Mode S Only all-call will trigger transponder replies of all mode A/C/S transponders.
dogru: Mode S Broadcast will transmit information to all mode S transponders.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: With mode-S each aircraft can be uniquely interrogated and identified by using the…
Special Position Identification (SPI) pulse.
24-bits address code, giving more than 16 million possible codes.
twelve-pulse train, giving 212 possible codes.
dogru: 24-bits address code, giving more than 16 million possible codes.

soru:
long (30.25 msec) P3 pulse.
Which statement is correct with respect to selections on the transponder control panel?
secenekler: The correct ICAO 24 bits aircraft address code must be inserted on the control panel.
With the selector knobs the four digits of the squawk can be selected using the decimal symbols 0 through 9.
With ALT selected a reply in the modes A, C and S can be transmitted.
With ON selected a reply in the modes A, C and S can be transmitted.
dogru: With ALT selected a reply in the modes A, C and S can be transmitted.

soru: Which of the following statements is correct about SSR?
secenekler: The selector knobs must be used to select digits 0 to 9 in order to squawk
The selector must be set to “ON” to transmit Mode C
The unique ICAO 24 bit identifier must be entered before  transmitting
The selector must be set to “ALT” to transmit Mode A,C and S
dogru: The selector must be set to “ALT” to transmit Mode A,C and S

soru: Which one of the following switch positions should be used when selecting a code on the Transponder?
secenekler: IDENT (Identification)
OFF
NORMAL
STBY (Standby)
dogru: STBY (Standby)

soru: To indicate radio failure, an aircraft SSR transponder must be set to…
In order to indicate unlawful interference with the operation of the flight, the aircraft SSR transponder must be set to…
The two main design functions of SSR Mode S are…
secenekler: collision avoidance using TCAS II and improved HF communication capability.
continuous automatic position reporting using GPS satellites and collision avoidance using TCAS II.
the elimination of ground to air communications and the introduction of automatic separation between aircraft using TCAS II.
air to ground and ground to air data link communications and improved ATC aircraft surveillance capability.
dogru: air to ground and ground to air data link communications and improved ATC aircraft surveillance capability.

soru: When an aircraft is operating its Secondary Surveillance Radar in Mode C an air traffic controller’s presentation gives information regarding the aircraft’s indicated pressure altitude in increments of:
secenekler: 150 ft
250 ft
200 ft
100 ft
dogru: 100 ft

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: In order to indicate an emergency situation, the aircraft SSR transponder must be set to…
The selection of code 7500 on an aircraft SSR transponder indicates…
an emergency.
radio communication failure.
unlawful interference with the operation of the flight.
dogru: unlawful interference with the operation of the flight.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: a transponder malfunction.
The selection of code 7600 on an aircraft SSR transponder indicates…
radio communication failure.
unlawful interference with the operation of the flight.
dogru: radio communication failure.

soru: a transponder malfunction.
secenekler: an emergency.
The selection of code 7700 on an aircraft SSR transponder indicates…
radio communication failure.
an emergency.
dogru: an emergency.

soru:
a transponder malfunction.
unlawful interference with the operation of the flight.
Garbling in mode A and C may occur:
secenekler: with two or more aircraft in different directions from the ground station, at the same altitude and a slant range of less than 1.7 NM.
when two or more aircraft are in approximately the same direction from the ground station with a difference in slant range of more than 1.7 NM.
with two or more aircraft in different directions from the interrogator, at the same altitude and a slant range of more than 1.7 NM.
when two or more aircraft are in approximately the same direction from the interrogator with a difference in slant range of less than 1.7 NM.
dogru: when two or more aircraft are in approximately the same direction from the interrogator with a difference in slant range of less than 1.7 NM.

soru: When switching on the weather radar, after start-up, a single very bright line appears on the screen. This means that the:
secenekler: Scanning of the cathode ray tube is faulty
Transmitter is faulty
Receiver is faulty
Scanner is not rotating
dogru: Scanning of the cathode ray tube is faulty

soru: A radar which employs an interrogator/transponder principle is:
secenekler: Primary Radar
Doppler Radar
Secondary Surveillance Radar
Continuous Wave Radar
dogru: Secondary Surveillance Radar

soru: The maximum range of a pulse radar is affected by:
secenekler: All of these
Pulse width
PRP
Transmission frequency
dogru: All of these

soru: The PRF of a primary pulse radar is 500. Its maximum unambiguous range in NM is approximately:
secenekler: 160 NM
600 NM
300 NM
320 NM
dogru: 160 NM

soru: What beam is used in the mapping mode of an airborne weather radar that can utilise two different beam shapes?
secenekler: A pencil beam effective up to a maximum range of 60 NM.
A pencil beam effective up to a range of 150 NM.
A cosecant squared beam effective up to a maximum of 50 NM to 60 NM.
A cosecant squared beam effective up to a range of 150 NM.
dogru: A cosecant squared beam effective up to a maximum of 50 NM to 60 NM.

soru: The allocation of 24-bit aircraft addresses is described in ICAO annex 10, it states that:
secenekler: Military aircraft will have been allocated with an ICAO Aircraft Address which is hard coded into the airframe
Civil aircraft will have been allocated with an ICAO Aircraft Address which is hard coded into the airframe
Every aircraft will have been allocated with a NATO Aircraft Address which is hard coded into the airframe
Every aircraft will have been allocated with an ICAO Aircraft Address which is hard coded into the airframe
dogru: Every aircraft will have been allocated with an ICAO Aircraft Address which is hard coded into the airframe

soru: Mode S - How does a ground interrogation signal (Mode A and C) get transmitted to a transponder?
secenekler: By using the long P4 pulse
In the form of pairs of pulses
In form of pairs of interrogative pulses with an additional control pulse
In the form of a series of pulses and a Special Position Identification (SPI) pulse
dogru: In form of pairs of interrogative pulses with an additional control pulse

soru: Which of the following weather returns displayed on an Airborne Weather Radar should be avoided?
secenekler: Columns, Anvils, Fingers
Fingers, Hooks, Scallops
Hooks, Pools, Iso-Echo
Pencils, Rods, Cones
dogru: Fingers, Hooks, Scallops

soru: Which of the folowing groups of shapes on an airborne weather radar display should be avoided?
secenekler: Cumulonimbus, TRS, Thunderstorms
Fingers, Hooks, Scallops
U-Bends, Fingers, Scallops
Fingers, Towering Cumulus, U-Shapes
dogru: Fingers, Hooks, Scallops

soru: A Mode S transponder receives among other pulses a long P4 pulse from an interrogator. What does this mean?
secenekler: The interrogator transmits a Mode A/C/S all call.
The interrogator transmits a Mode S only all call.
The interrogator transmits a Mode A only all call.
The interrogator transmits a Mode A/C only all call.
dogru: The interrogator transmits a Mode A/C/S all call.

soru: What does it mean if a transponder receives a stronger P2 pulse than P1 pulse?
secenekler: a side lobe is being received
the P1 pulse is valid
a primary lobe is being received
the P1 pulse is valid and will be responded to
dogru: a side lobe is being received

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: What is the advantage of a primary radar with MTI over one that is without.
Doppler effect is used to determine aircraft true air speed
Ground clutter can be eliminated
Doppler effect is used to determine aircraft ground speed
dogru: Ground clutter can be eliminated

soru:
Moving targets are eliminated
Regarding Airborne Weather Radar, which of the following statements is correct?
secenekler: Narrow colour bands indicate less severe weather
Areas of clear air turbulence can be detected.
Thunderstorms may not be visible behind other heavy showers.
It is safe to fly through a finger-like extension from the main radar return.
dogru: Thunderstorms may not be visible behind other heavy showers.

soru: In what circumstances will an aircraft transponder receive a P2 pulse that is stronger than the P1 or P3 pulse?
secenekler: The ground station is requesting a mode "C" response.
The aircraft is out of range of the SSR ground transmitter.
The aircraft is located in the side lobe pulse zone.
The aircraft is located in the primary pulse zone.
dogru: The aircraft is located in the side lobe pulse zone.

soru: In order to provide an Air Traffic Services (ATS) surveillance service, what minimum information must be provided to the controller on the surveillance system display?  Indication of position and also...
secenekler: level, speed, vertical speed, an overlay of the terrain, and (when available) the aircraft identification and destination
an overlay of the terrain, and the aircraft identification, speed, vertical speed, and level.
any map information needed to provide the surveillance service, aircraft speed, and (when available) the aircraft identification and level.
any map information needed to provide the surveillance service, and (when available) the aircraft identification and level.
dogru: any map information needed to provide the surveillance service, and (when available) the aircraft identification and level.

soru: What is an advantage of an SSR with a ‘selective addressing mode’ over primary radar?
secenekler: It allows for unambiguous aircraft identification.
Its range is unrelated to its transmitting power.
It is independent from airborne-and space-based systems.
In case of a transponder malfunction, the SSR can be used as a primary radar.
dogru: It allows for unambiguous aircraft identification.

soru: Which of the following additional aircraft parameters can be downloaded by the Mode S transponder?
secenekler: Vertical rate, Track angle rate, True track angle
TAS, Pitch attitude, Magnetic track angle
Pitch angle, vertical rate, TAS
Vertical rate, selected heading, TAS
dogru: Vertical rate, Track angle rate, True track angle

soru: What is the accuracy of Mode S altitude reporting?
secenekler: 50 feet
25 feet
100 feet
200 feet
dogru: 25 feet

soru:
With thunderstorms reported in the vicinity, when lining up on the runway what would you do with the weather radar?
View Annex 620798
secenekler: After scanning upwards to look for weather set the tilt a few degrees up
Turn the Ground clutter switch to off
Increase the gain to get a better image
Select map mode so get a wider coverage during the take-off period
dogru: After scanning upwards to look for weather set the tilt a few degrees up

soru: Why should Airborne Weather Radar (AWR) systems be used with extreme caution when on the ground?
secenekler: The radar antenna can be easily damaged during ground-taxi manoeuvres (especially on uneven taxiways).
People could be exposed to Super High Frequency (SHF) radiation which, if absorbed in large enough amounts, can lead to tissue damage.
The intense returns from the ground and surrounding terrain will not allow accurate identification of potentially hazardous weather when lined up for departure.
The radar system may overheat and shut down due to the intensity of the returns from the ground, buildings, and surrounding terrain.
dogru: People could be exposed to Super High Frequency (SHF) radiation which, if absorbed in large enough amounts, can lead to tissue damage.

soru: Which of the following modes, which are selectable on the Mode Control Panel (MCP) / Flight Control Unit (FCU), provides a parameter that is downlinked from a Mode S Enhanced Surveillance (EHS) transponder?
secenekler: Selected Vertical Speed
Selected Course
Selected Altitude
Selected Heading
dogru: Selected Altitude

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Select three options that represent data that can be provided in an SSR response with EHS 1.Vertical rate 2.True heading 3.Magnetic heading 4.EAS 5.Groundspeed 6.Pitch attitude (Quadrant style originally)

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: If the airborne weather radar system is fitted with an auto-tilt function, what does that mean?The radar antenna tilt is automatically adjusted…
based on the altitude of the aircraft.
to tilt fully down when taxiing to reduce the possibility of injury to ground personnel.
dogru: based on the altitude of the aircraft.

soru:
based on the attitude of the aircraft.
to track the areas with the greatest intensity of precipitation.
How does the Airborne Weather Radar (AWR) detect wind shear?
secenekler: The aircraft’s groundspeed is measured in the MAP mode and this is compared to the airspeed.
The change in intensity of detectable precipitation is measured
The Doppler shift of particles of dust, sand, and soot is measured
The Doppler shift of detectable precipitation is measured
dogru: The Doppler shift of detectable precipitation is measured

soru: Why should weather radar be tilted down when looking for thunderstorms?
secenekler: Snow and dry hail is what is picked up on radar and this is mostly present at lower altiudes
Wet hail and rain is what is picked up on radar and this is mostly present at lower altiudes
to reduce ground clutter
to avoid returns from ice crystals in the cloud tops
dogru: Wet hail and rain is what is picked up on radar and this is mostly present at lower altiudes

soru: When using the AWR in the climb how should the scanner tilt be set?
secenekler: tilted so that the lower part of the radar beam is roughly parallel to the earth horizontal
tilt up
tilt down
zero tilt
dogru: tilt up

soru: Why should the tilt of an Airborne Weather Radar system (without auto tilt) be changed to a higher setting when descending over terrain?
secenekler: If the beam’s tilt remains at the setting used during cruise, the scanning range will be reduced and the strength of the reflected signals will be weaker.
The higher tilt setting will narrow the beam width, allowing for effective detection of cumulonimbus clouds at shorter range.
If the beam’s tilt remains at the setting used during cruise, the scanning range will be reduced and significant ground clutter may be displayed.
The higher tilt setting will keep the beam above the aircraft’s flight path.
dogru: If the beam’s tilt remains at the setting used during cruise, the scanning range will be reduced and significant ground clutter may be displayed.

soru: What are the advantages of SSR technology?
secenekler: Ability to download extra information from the aircraft
A cheaper system to run
Allows aircraft to fly closer to each other
More accurate position fixes
dogru: Ability to download extra information from the aircraft

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Which of the items listed below are additional aircraft parameters that are downlinked from Mode S Enhanced Surveillance (EHS) transponders? 1. Pitch angle 2. Vertical rate 3. Track angle rate or true airspeed 4. True track angle
2, 3, and 4 only.
2 and 4 only.
1, 2, and 3 only.
dogru: 2, 3, and 4 only.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: 1, 3, and 4 only.

Which of the items listed below are additional aircraft parameters that are downlinked from Mode S Enhanced Surveillance (EHS) transponders? 1. Groundspeed 2. Magnetic heading 3. Equivalent airspeed 4. True heading 5. Vertical rate

soru:
1, 3, and 4
2, 3, and 5
Refer to the diagram of a weather radar control panel. What should be considered when lined up on the departure runway and there are thunderstorms in the vicinity?
View Annex 620899
secenekler: The ground clutter suppression (GND CTLR SPRS) switch should be set to OFF to eliminate confusion with weather returns.
The radar should be tilted up (UP) (rotary switch 2) to check for any significant precipitation on the climb-out path.
The gain control (rotary switch 3) should be set to minimum (MIN) to ensure that all returns are displayed.
The radar should be tilted down (DN) (rotary switch 2) to ensure ground returns are displayed to verify that the system is working.
dogru: The radar should be tilted up (UP) (rotary switch 2) to check for any significant precipitation on the climb-out path.

soru: Which data can be determined and displayed by ATC Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) and Primary Surveillance Radar (PSR) systems?
secenekler: PSR: Target range, bearing, and aircraft altitude. SSR: Target range and bearing, aircraft identification codes, aircraft height, selective addressing, and full two-way data link.
PSR: Target range, bearing, and aircraft identification. SSR: Aircraft identification codes, aircraft altitude, selective addressing, and full two-way data link.
PSR: Target range. SSR: Target range and bearing, aircraft altitude and height, selective addressing, and full two-way data link.
PSR: Target range and bearing. SSR: Target range and bearing, aircraft identification codes, aircraft altitude, selective addressing, and full two-way data link.
dogru: PSR: Target range and bearing. SSR: Target range and bearing, aircraft identification codes, aircraft altitude, selective addressing, and full two-way data link.

soru:
Refer to the diagram of a weather radar control panel. Selection of WX+T mode on rotary switch 1 will help to detect and display areas of…
View Annex 620909
secenekler: CAT within precipitation by detecting the Doppler shift of the reflected radar signal.
CAT within precipitation by detecting the Doppler shift of the falling droplets
turbulence within precipitation by detecting the Doppler shift of the reflected radar signal
turbulence within precipitation by detecting the Doppler shift of the falling droplets
dogru: turbulence within precipitation by detecting the Doppler shift of the reflected radar signal

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: What information may an ATS controller provide when only using his/her radar situation display? Information about…
any observed aircraft on a conflicting path.
the latest data from airborne weather radar systems.
conflicting traffic detected by ACAS (Airborne Collision Avoidance System)
dogru: any observed aircraft on a conflicting path.

soru:
the optimum altitude to improve aircraft performance
Sometimes an SSR ground station receives (from a certain aircraft) the reply pulses caused by interrogations from other SSR ground stations. What is the name of this effect?
secenekler: Fruiting
Code garbling
Selective addressing
SPI
dogru: Fruiting

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: An airborne transponder can provide coded reply signals in response to interrogation signals from
ground secondary surveillance radar and from primary ground radar.
ground secondary surveillance radar and from aircraft equipped with a mode A and C transponder.
ground secondary surveillance radar and from aircraft equipped with TCAS.
dogru: ground secondary surveillance radar and from aircraft equipped with TCAS.

soru:
primary ground radar and from aircraft equipped with a transponder.
What is the function of pulse P2 in mode A/C interrogation?
secenekler: To determine the interrogation mode.
To avoid responses from aircraft that are located in the direction of the side lobes of the interrogator antenna.
To avoid code garbling if two aircraft are located in the same direction -as seen from the interrogator- and at a distance of less than approximately 1.7 NM apart.
To avoid fruiting if an aircraft is interrogated by more than one SSR interrogator at the same time.
dogru: To avoid responses from aircraft that are located in the direction of the side lobes of the interrogator antenna.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: In relation to primary radar, pulse repetition frequency means the…
radar frequency used.
number of pulses transmitted per second.
time between each transmission of pulses.
dogru: number of pulses transmitted per second.

soru:
number of revolutions performed by the radar antenna per minute.
Using the pulse technique, what determines the minimum range and the maximum unambiguous range of a primary radar?
secenekler: Minimum range: pulse length, maximum range: pulse repetition frequency
Minimum range: rotation speed of antenna, maximum range: length of the time base
Minimum range: transmission frequency, maximum range: transmitter power output
Minimum range: transmission frequency, maximum range: size of reflecting surface
dogru: Minimum range: pulse length, maximum range: pulse repetition frequency

soru: Where is pulse modulation used?
secenekler: In ILS localiser.
In VHF.
In VOR.
In Radar.
dogru: In Radar.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The airborne weather radar…
may receive no reflections from a thunderstorm in an area behind a heavy rain shower.
uses circular polarisation in order to reduce echoes of heavy showers.
has the possibility to detect clear air turbulence behind a thunder storm by measuring phase differences caused by the Doppler effect.
dogru: may receive no reflections from a thunderstorm in an area behind a heavy rain shower.

soru:
has a second transmitter working on a lower frequency in order to penetrate rain showers.
What is the principle behind the windshear warning capability of an Airborne Weather Radar?
secenekler: The change in intensity of detectable precipitation is measured.
The Doppler shift of particles of dust, sand, and soot is measured.
The Doppler shift of detectable precipitation is measured.
The aircraft’s groundspeed is measured in the MAP mode and this is compared to the airspeed.
dogru: The Doppler shift of detectable precipitation is measured.

soru: Moving Target Indicator (MTI) in ground radar is used to:
secenekler: emphasise all echoes travelling faster than a certain speed selected by the operator.
emphasise the difference between slow and fast moving echoes.
suppress all stationary echoes.
assess the travelling speed of moving echoes.
dogru: suppress all stationary echoes.

soru: What advantage does a primary radar system fitted with an MTI capability have over a system that is not?
secenekler: The Doppler Shift is used to determine the aircraft’s groundspeed.
Moving targets are eliminated from the controller’s display.
Ground clutter is eliminated from the controller’s display.
The Doppler Shift is used to determine the aircraft’s True Airspeed (TAS).
dogru: Ground clutter is eliminated from the controller’s display.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Name a significant advantage of Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) system over a primary radar system-
There are no side lobes during transmission.
The user gets additional information e.g. altitude.
There is no special equipment necessary for SSR.
dogru: The user gets additional information e.g. altitude.

soru:
The user gets a higher level of accuracy for bearings and distances.
Which radar system can be used to detect aircraft that are not equipped with an operating transponder in flight?
secenekler: Active Transponder (AT) Radar System
Multilateral Surveillance (MLAT) Radar System
Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR)
Primary Surveillance Radar (PSR)
dogru: Primary Surveillance Radar (PSR)

soru: Which statement about code garbling is correct?
secenekler: Code garbling occurs at the SSR ground stations if from an aircraft the response pulses to more than one simultaneous interrogations are received.
Code garbling occurs at the transponder if the interrogation pulses of two different SSR ground stations are received.
Code garbling occurs at the transponder if the transponder is not able to distinguish between a Mode A and a Mode C interrogation.
Code garbling occurs at the SSR ground station if the mode A/C replies of two different aircraft overlap (in time).
dogru: Code garbling occurs at the SSR ground station if the mode A/C replies of two different aircraft overlap (in time).

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: To determine if a return is from a cloud at or above the height of the aircraft, the tilt control of an Airborne Weather Radar (AWR) with a 5° beam width should be set to…
5° up.

soru:
2.5° down.
What determines the operating mode (A or C) of the SSR transponder?
secenekler: The position of the SPI pulse.
The difference in amplitude of the pulses P1/P3 and P2.
The travel time of the pulses transmitted by the interrogator.
The time interval between the pulses P1 and P3.
dogru: The time interval between the pulses P1 and P3.

soru: What is (one of) the differences between a Mode A/C/S all call and a mode A/C only all call?
secenekler: The length of the pulse P4.
The presence of Mode A/C addresses.
The time interval between the pulses P1 and P3.
The presence of Mode S addresses.
dogru: The length of the pulse P4.

soru: A Mode C-transponder is able to report the pressure altitude in:
secenekler: 25 ft increments.
100 m increments.
50 m increments.
100 ft increments.
dogru: 100 ft increments.

soru: A mode A/C transponder receives a P2 pulse from which the amplitude is smaller than that of the P1 (and P3) pulse. What is the matter?
secenekler: The transponder is located in the direction of one of the side lobes of the interrogator antenna.
The interrogation is only meant for mode S transponders.
The transponder is beyond the usable range of the SSR ground station.
The transponder is located in the direction of the main lobe of the interrogator antenna.
dogru: The transponder is located in the direction of the main lobe of the interrogator antenna.

soru: A mode A/C transponder receives a P2 pulse from which the amplitude is larger than that of the P1 (and P3) pulse. What is the matter?
secenekler: The transponder is beyond the usable range of the SSR ground station.
The transponder is located in the direction of one of the side lobes of the interrogator antenna.
The transponder is located in the direction of the main lobe of the interrogator antenna.
The interrogation is only meant for mode S transponders.
dogru: The transponder is located in the direction of one of the side lobes of the interrogator antenna.

soru: A Mode S transponder receives among other pulses a short P4 pulse from an interrogator. What does this mean?
secenekler: The interrogator transmits a Mode A only all call.
The interrogator transmits a Mode A/C/S all call.
The interrogator transmits a Mode S only all call.
The interrogator transmits a Mode A/C only all call.
dogru: The interrogator transmits a Mode A/C only all call.

soru: Why should the tilt of an Airborne Weather Radar be lowered whilst searching for thunderstorms in high-level cruise flight?
secenekler: The greatest reflectivity occurs from dry hail and snow, and these elements exist mainly in the lower levels of a thunderstorm.
The greatest reflectivity occurs from wet hail and rain, and these elements exist mainly in the lower levels of a thunderstorm.
The ground clutter enhances the display by automatically turning up the gain and increasing the likelihood of detecting thunderstorm activity.
The ground clutter is more evident and therefore less likely to be confused with thunderstorm activity.
dogru: The greatest reflectivity occurs from wet hail and rain, and these elements exist mainly in the lower levels of a thunderstorm.

soru: Why can Secondary Surveillance Radar (SSR) systems provide ATC with more information than Primary Surveillance Radar (PSR) systems?
secenekler: ATC SSR systems have Moving Target Indicator (MTI) capability, which eliminates ground clutter and allows more aircraft to be tracked.
ATC SSR systems can reply to an aircraft interrogation and offer to provide the requested information.
As ATC SSR systems continuously interrogate aircraft within range, tracking is uninterrupted.
ATC SSR systems can interrogate an aircraft transponder and elicit replies.
dogru: ATC SSR systems can interrogate an aircraft transponder and elicit replies.

soru:
Refer to the diagram of a weather radar control panel. Increasing the gain on rotary switch number 3 increases the…
View Annex 621096
secenekler: beam width.
range of the display.
sensitivity of the receiver.
power of the radar.
dogru: sensitivity of the receiver.

soru: Which statement about the Special Position Identification (SPI) pulse is correct?
secenekler: This pulse is transmitted automatically every time the transponder responds to a mode A interrogation.
This pulse is transmitted by the SSR-interrogator if it is suspected that responses are received from aircraft which are located in the direction of the side lobes of the interrogator antenna.
This pulse is transmitted by the transponder as a result of the pilot pushing the IDENT button.
This pulse is transmitted by the SSR-interrogator in order to request for identification of a certain aircraft.
dogru: This pulse is transmitted by the transponder as a result of the pilot pushing the IDENT button.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: What are the potential dangers of operating an Airborne Weather Radar (AWR) on the ground? Radiated energy can…
disturb radio communications with ATC.
disturb nearby airport navigation systems.
harm human beings.
dogru: harm human beings.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: cause misalignment of the aircraft’s Air Data and Inertial Reference System -ADIRS-.
With reference to Mode S transponders, which statement is correct? The reported altitude data…
could be accurate to 25 ft resolution but it is dependent on the altitude sensor systems on board the aircraft.
must be accurate to 100 ft resolution and be independent of the altitude sensor systems on board the aircraft.
dogru: could be accurate to 25 ft resolution but it is dependent on the altitude sensor systems on board the aircraft.

soru:
could be accurate to 25 ft resolution and is independent of the altitude sensor systems on board the aircraft.
must be accurate to 25 ft resolution and be dependent on the altitude sensor systems on board the aircraft.
The task of Moving Target Indication (MTI) in a primary radar is to:
secenekler: Minimise the transmitted pulse length.
Enable staggered pulse repetition.
Transmit the velocity of aircraft.
Erase all stationary targets.
dogru: Erase all stationary targets.

soru: Which of the following statements describes an advantage of SSR over primary radar?
secenekler: SSR relies only on the reception of a reflected pulse of a radar target.
SSR allows the identification of aircraft without a working mode A/C/S transponder.
SSR is resistant to garbling and fruiting.
SSR allows the identification of a primary radar target by its code and call sign.
dogru: SSR allows the identification of a primary radar target by its code and call sign.

soru: In the descent it is important to set the tilt setting to a higher setting. Why is this?
secenekler: To avoid clutter from ground returns
to avoid scanning below the cloud layer and missing turbulence/weather
to scan the higher altitude clouds to avoid hail
During the descent the AWR is not required
dogru: To avoid clutter from ground returns

soru: AWR uses Doppler shift to:
secenekler: Calculate the movement of the weather relative to the aircraft position
Calculate the vertical extent of clouds
Calculate the distance to terrain
calculate turbulence by measuring the motion of water droplets
dogru: calculate turbulence by measuring the motion of water droplets

soru: A weather radar, set to the 100 NM scale, shows a squall between 40 NM and 50 NM.By changing the scale to 50 NM, the return on the radar screen should...
secenekler: decrease in area and move to the top of the screen.
decrease in area but not change in position on the screen.
increase in area and appear nearer to the bottom of the screen.
increase in area and move to the top of the screen.
dogru: increase in area and move to the top of the screen.
